data.title: IonQ appoints Inder Singh as CFO and COO
data.description:
Quantum computing and networking company IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) announced on Thursday the appointment of Inder Singh as CFO and COO, effective immediately.

Singh succeeds Thomas Kramer, who is set to remain with the company in an advisory capacity for up to 60 days.

Singh has stepped down from the board.

Most recently, he served as CFO of Arm, a British semiconductor and software design company.

data.title: Palantir partners with Lumen, expands Lear Corp. pact
data.description:
• Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) and Lumen Technologies (NYSE:LUMN) are  partnering to bring Palantir’s Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform to Lumen as it shifts from a traditional telecom to a next-gen tech infrastructure company.
  • The tools will help Lumen streamline operations, improve decision-making, and modernize systems. The collaboration is designed to boost efficiency and speed Lumen’s transformation into an AI-ready, cloud-powered network provider.
  • LUMN +6.7% premarket to $5.04.
  • Separately, Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) announced a five-year expansion of its partnership with Lear Corp. (NYSE:LEA), as part of which Lear will broaden its use of Palantir Foundry, as well as Palantir’s Warp Speed manufacturing operating system and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) across its global manufacturing footprint.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DeepSeek plans year-end launch of AI agent to compete with OpenAI - report
data.description:
• DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) is developing an artificial intelligence model with more advanced AI agent features to compete with U.S. rivals like OpenAI, Bloomberg reported on Thursday.
  • The Hangzhou-based startup is building an AI model that’s designed to carry out multi-step actions on a person’s behalf with minimal direction from the user, the report said. The system is also meant to learn and improve based on its prior actions.
  • The Chinese startup, which stunned the world in January, officially launched its updated DeepSeek-V3.1 AI model last month, which surpasses its R1 model on key benchmarks.
  • Chinese AI firms, including DeepSeek, have ramped up development of advanced language models. The rivalry highlights escalating tech tensions, with China aiming to challenge U.S. dominance in the global AI race.

data.title: G-III Apparel Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25, revenue of $613.3M
data.description:
• G-III Apparel press release (NASDAQ:GIII): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25.
  • Revenue of $613.3M (-4.9% Y/Y).
  • Outlook: Fiscal 2026

    Net sales are expected to be approximately $3.02 billion. This compares to net sales of $3.18 billion for fiscal 2025.

    Net income is expected to be between $112.0 million and $122.0 million, or diluted earnings per share between $2.53 and $2.73. This compares to net income of $193.6 million, or $4.20 per diluted share, for fiscal 2025.

    Non-GAAP net income for fiscal 2026 is expected to be between $113.0 million and $123.0 million, or diluted earnings per share between $2.55 and $2.75. This compares to non-GAAP net income of $203.6 million, or diluted earnings per share of $4.42 for fiscal 2025.

    Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2026 is expected to be between $198.0 million and $208.0 million compared to adjusted EBITDA of $325.9 million in fiscal 2025.

data.title: Brady Non-GAAP EPS of $1.26, revenue of $397.3M
data.description:
• Brady  press release (NYSE:BRC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.26.
  • Revenue of $397.3M (+15.7% Y/Y).
  • Announced diluted EPS guidance for the year ending July 31, 2026 at a range of $4.55 to $4.85 on a GAAP basis, and a range of $4.85 to $5.15 on an adjusted basis.

data.title: offering
data.description:
• American Critical Minerals (OTCPK:APCOF) plans to raise up to $1 million through a non-brokered private placement of up to 5 million units at $0.20 each. Each unit includes one common share and half a warrant, with full warrants exercisable at $0.30 for 24 months.
  • Proceeds will support development of the Green River Project, including exploration work for lithium and bromine, updating its technical report, paying concession and regulatory fees, marketing, and general working capital.

data.title: Sanofi falls after late-stage trial data for eczema therapy
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) ADRs fell ~10% in the premarket on Thursday after data from a late-stage trial for amlitelimab, its experimental therapy for the inflammatory skin condition atopic dermatitis (often referred to as eczema), disappointed investors.

The French drugmaker said that the global Phase 3 trial named COAST 1, which tested two dosing regimens of the monoclonal antibody, met all primary and key secondary endpoints. However, there will be questions over how the injectable will stack up against blockbuster eczema drugs, such as Dupixent, which Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) markets with Regeneron (REGN).

According to the company, amlitelimab dosed either every four weeks or every 12 weeks reached all primary and key secondary endpoints related to skin clearance and disease severity, with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful impact against placebo at Week 24.

COAST 1, one of five clinical trials supporting the company’s OCEANA development program for amlitelimab in AD, enrolled more than 600 patients aged 12 years and older with moderate-to-severe AD.

Regarding safety, the Paris-based drugmaker said treatment-emergent adverse events were more common in the placebo arm, and the rates of treatment discontinuations due to TEAEs were similar across the placebo arm and amlitelimab arms.

Other trials in the OCEANA program, namely SHORE, COAST 2, AQUA, and ESTUARY, which, along with COAST 1, will support global regulatory submissions for amlitelimab for AD, are expected to generate data through 2026.

data.title: Shoe Carnival gains after pointing to strong back-to-school results in August
data.description:
Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) shot up in early trading on Thursday after the retailer issued a mixed second quarter earnings report. 

Revenue fell 7.9% from the same quarter a year ago to $306.4 million. Comparable sales declined 7.5%, including a high single-digit decline at Shoe Carnival and break-even results at Shoe Station.

CEO Mark Worden noted that as Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) moved into the back-to-school period in early August, execution hit a higher level. "We delivered positive comparable store sales for the company and margin expansion across all banners during the period that drives approximately 25 percent of our annual profits. This return to growth during our highest-stakes season - ahead of our projected timeline - validates that our transformation is accelerating," he highlighted.

By banner, Shoe Station grew comparable sales high-single digits in fiscal August, driven by high single-digit growth in the children's category and low-twenties growth in the adult athletics category with margin expansion. Meanwhile, Shoe Carnival achieved positive children's category comparable sales with margin growth while maintaining discipline in other categories.

Shoe Carnival's (SCVL) gross profit margin rate increased 270 basis points to 38.8% of sales. The margin expansion was said to demonstrate the power of the company's rebanner strategy. Merchandise margin improved 390 basis points, driven by disciplined pricing across all banners, a favorable mix shift toward merchandise preferred by Shoe Station's higher-income customers, and strategic inventory investments. That improvement more than offset 120 basis points of deleverage in buying, distribution, and occupancy costs.

At the end of the quarter, Shoe Carnival (SCVL) operated 428 stores: 313 Shoe Carnival stores, 87 Shoe Station stores, and 28 Rogan's stores. The Shoe Station store count has more than doubled since the second quarter of 2024.

Looking ahead, Shoe Carnival (SCVL) expects full-year revenue of $1.12 billion to $1.15 billion (midpoint $1.135 billion) vs. $1.16 billion consensus. The company sees full-year EPS of $1.70 to $2.10 (midpoint $1.90) vs. a prior outlook for $1.60 to $2.10 and the consensus estimate of $1.79.

Shares of Shoe Carnival (SCVL) were up 7.4% in premarket action.
